request.name: Validate API key
request.description: Calls GET /v1/account to confirm your API key is valid. On success, the test script confirms the key and prints the account name. Use this as the first request after pasting your apiKey into the environment.

description.content: Account metadata for the authenticated API key.
Retrieve the account the API key belongs to.

description.content: Cursor-paginated list of messages in a chat. Supports free-text search (`query`), date range (`since`/`until`), and message-kind filtering (`message_types`). Requires `chats:read`.

query.description: Result ordering hint. Default `latest` (newest-first).
Free-text search across message bodies and captions.
Max messages to return.
Pagination cursor from a previous response.
Lower bound (ISO 8601). Only messages with timestamp >= since.
Upper bound (ISO 8601). Only messages with timestamp <= until.

Filter to specific message kinds. Comma-separated or repeated. Useful for "all PDFs" (`document`) or "images today" (`image`). System events (gp2/revoked/newsletter_notification) are excluded by default unless explicitly listed here.

query.description: Max participants to return.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Clears the unread badge on the connected engine for the given chat. Requires `chats:write`.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

Brings the chat to the foreground on the connected WhatsApp Web client (creates the chat if it doesn`t exist yet for the engine). Useful before issuing follow-up reads on a fresh JID. Requires `chats:write`.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Returns the WhatsApp ack value for a sent message: -1=error, 0=pending, 1=server, 2=device, 3=read, 4=played. Requires `chats:read`.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Add or replace your reaction to a message. Pass an empty `emoji` string to remove. Requires `chats:write`.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

Asks the engine to pull older history from the connected phone for chats that haven`t been fully synced yet. Use this once before paginating with `since` if you need messages older than what`s already cached. Requires `chats:read`.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

Returns a hosted URL for the message media without inlining bytes. Faster + cheaper than `media` when the caller can fetch the URL themselves. Same `media_unavailable` semantics. Requires `chats:read`.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Get delivery status for up to 200 sent messages in one call. Useful for campaign dashboards / status reconciliation. Requires `chats:read`.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

Download the media attached to a WhatsApp message (image, video, document, audio).
Returns either a hosted URL (`url`) or inline `data_base64`, plus mimetype + filename.

CAVEAT: for own-sent newsletter media (messages you sent to a `@newsletter` chat), the bytes returned are a WA-generated preview JPEG (~7KB) rather than the original you uploaded — WA releases the original blob from memory immediately after the unencrypted upload. The response will include `original_quality:false` when this fallback is in effect; the caller can warn the user or decide to retry from a different source.

Requires `chats:read`.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

description.content: Send or retrieve WhatsApp messages.
List messages sent through the API, newest first (cursor-paginated).

Send a message via WhatsApp. The body is a discriminated union — set the `type` field to one of `text`, `media`, or `poll`.

**Variants:**

- `type: "text"` — required `text` (1–4096 chars). Example body:
  ```json
  {"type":"text","to":"+15551234567","text":"hello"}
  ```
- `type: "media"` — required `media.url` (HTTPS). Optional `media.kind` (`image` · `video` · `audio` · `document` · `sticker` · `voice`; auto-detected when omitted), `media.caption`, `media.filename`. Example:
  ```json
  {"type":"media","to":"+15551234567","media":{"url":"https://cdn.example.com/x.pdf","kind":"document","filename":"receipt.pdf"}}
  ```
- `type: "poll"` — required `poll.question` and `poll.options` (2–12 items). Optional `poll.allow_multiple` (default false). Example:
  ```json
  {"type":"poll","to":"+15551234567","poll":{"question":"Pizza?","options":["Yes","No"]}}
  ```

All variants accept optional `send_at` (ISO 8601 ≥10s future, ≤365d), `from` (E.164 sender for multi-session workspaces), and `reply_to` (wire `key` of a prior message to quote).

See [Sending messages](/docs/messages) for runnable Python / Node / PHP / cURL examples per variant.

info.name: Blueticks API
info.description:
Auto-generated from openapi.json. Import a Blueticks Postman environment (Production or Staging) alongside this collection, paste your API key into the env's `apiKey` field, and every request authenticates automatically.

Need a key? Generate one at https://www.blueticks.co/dashboard.
